rules: Has formal childcare experience (daycare, preschool, classroom teacher, para, substitute, afterschool, Head Start, camp counselor) in any amount
Located in Utah, Georgia, or Arizona (confirmed by stated location or phone area code)
Available part-time or full-time
Willing to drive any reasonable distance
Completed all VideoAsk questions with substantive answers

approve_medium.description: Approve with MEDIUM confidence — Erica should glance
rules: Has formal childcare experience but limited (less than 1 year)
Located in-market but in a less-served area
Mixed experience (some formal, some informal)
Schedule has some constraints but generally available

flag.description: FLAG for Erica's review — needs human judgment
rules: Only informal experience (babysitting, nannying, own children — no formal setting)
Location unclear or borderline
Short-term availability (summer only, leaving area)
Relocating to the area (not there yet)
Very brief or garbled transcript responses
Conflict resolution answer raises concerns
Under-25 with minimal experience
Out-of-market area code but claims to be in-market
Arizona candidate with less than 6 months formal experience
Arizona candidate whose only experience is nannying (does not count as professional experience per AZ regulations — must be child care center, daycare, or preschool)
Candidate claims experience but gives no specifics — no employer name, no setting, no concrete details (vague experience)

never_auto_deny.description: NEVER auto-deny — always FLAG for human decision
never_auto_deny.note: Denials trigger rejection email + text to teacher. Non-reversible. All denials are human decisions.
flag_instead: Clearly out of market (different state, no plans to move)
Mentions inability to pass background check
Major red flags in conflict resolution answer

arizona.min_experience: Strict — 6 months in a child care center, daycare, or preschool required
arizona.notes: State DHS licensing requirements. Nannying is NOT considered professional experience per AZ regulations. Must be center-based (child care center, daycare, preschool). No babysitting, nannying, or informal care.

erica_custom_rules.rule: Arizona candidates must have specifically worked in a child care center, daycare, or preschool to be approved. Nannying is NOT considered professional experience per AZ regulations. This is AZ-specific only.

male_candidates.description: Flag male candidates for placement consideration
male_candidates.note: Many childcare centers have gender-based placement policies. Male candidates are not auto-denied but flagged so Erica can factor center preferences into her decision.
male_candidates.action: Add 👨 flag to Red Flags column. Erica decides based on available center placements.

evaluation_examples.description: Real examples of good vs bad candidates from the 2026-03-30 batch. Use these to calibrate evaluations.

strong_approve.name: Chelsea Veal
strong_approve.why: Bachelor's in ECE (2020), master's in ECE (2023), pursuing ECE specialist degree. Currently a full-time teacher. Three ECE degrees = massively overqualified.

strong_approve.name: Shana Birchfield
strong_approve.why: 20 years higher education + 5 years elementary. Supply teacher and permanent substitute in Marietta City School District. Overqualified.

strong_approve.name: Linda Arnold
strong_approve.why: 10+ years daycare + 3 years 2nd grade teaching + additional grades. Retired educator. Massively overqualified.

strong_approve.name: Mia Bland
strong_approve.why: 8+ years ABA behavioral technician (18mo-17yr, clinic/home/school) + 3 years instructional aide. Easily exceeds Arizona's strict 6-month bar.

strong_approve.name: Kendra Jones
strong_approve.why: 10 years early childhood education + 14 years medical field + currently Boys & Girls Club team coordinator in Lawrenceville.

strong_approve.name: Oyanioye Eke
strong_approve.why: Literally grew up in a daycare. 4+ years hands-on experience with all ages up to 14. Jonesboro, GA. Full-time.

strong_approve.name: Terri Abrams
strong_approve.why: 37 years in childcare. Head Start center director, Early Head Start director, afterschool coordinator, classroom teacher. Exceptional — only answered Q3 but that's enough.

solid_approve.name: Keyonna Anthony
solid_approve.why: Multiple daycares and preschools (infants through preschool), pursuing elementary education degree. In-market south Atlanta. 10-15mi drive.

solid_approve.name: Makiya Meredith
solid_approve.why: 4 years early childcare at 2 daycares (1.5yr at one, 2+ years at Pride & Joy). Studying elementary education. Conyers, GA.

solid_approve.name: Brian Long
solid_approve.why: Head Start school — started as floater, worked up to TA, worked with autistic kids ages 3-5. Augusta, GA. 👨 Male but strong formal experience.

solid_approve.name: Deyanira Szymanski
solid_approve.why: 10 years experience ages 3 through 6th grade, bilingual English/Spanish, field trip experience. Queen Creek, AZ. Easily meets AZ bar.

judgment_calls_flag.name: Breanna McGee
judgment_calls_flag.why: Nursing student with years of babysitting since age 12, but NO formal childcare setting. Healthcare background shows responsibility. Georgia prefers formal.
judgment_calls_flag.note: Erica might approve based on nursing/healthcare maturity or deny based on no formal setting

judgment_calls_flag.name: Angel Mills
judgment_calls_flag.why: Explicitly says 'I do not currently have any previous childcare experience.' Has healthcare training (medical admin, nurse's aide). Only answered 1/5 questions.
judgment_calls_flag.note: No childcare experience + healthcare pivot. Most likely a deny but Erica decides.

judgment_calls_flag.name: Jaiden Aliff
judgment_calls_flag.why: 7 years as a professional nanny, Mercer University grad. Extensive but technically informal. Georgia prefers formal.
judgment_calls_flag.note: 7 years of nannying is significant — Erica might treat professional nannying as formal enough

judgment_calls_flag.name: Jessica Grant
judgment_calls_flag.why: Claims 8+ years caring for 20+ children ages 2mo-8yr, but describes it as 'babysitting and raising a child of my own.' Extensive volume but informal.
judgment_calls_flag.note: The volume (8yr, 20+ kids) is notable even if informal

clear_flags_likely_deny.name: Drue Coats
clear_flags_likely_deny.why: Out of work for almost a year, says childcare is 'something kind of fun' and 'new to me.' Zero childcare experience. Arizona requires 6mo formal.

clear_flags_likely_deny.name: Jarvis Frazier
clear_flags_likely_deny.why: 24, in police academy, looking for part-time side job. Zero childcare experience mentioned. 👨 Male.

clear_flags_likely_deny.name: Naeja Ellis
clear_flags_likely_deny.why: Q3: 'Maybe I might. Do I want to work?' — barely coherent, low effort. Not a serious candidate.

clear_flags_likely_deny.name: Hermelind Aguilar
clear_flags_likely_deny.why: No childcare experience, just has a 16-month-old daughter. Arizona requires 6mo formal — having your own toddler doesn't count.

data_insufficient.name: Eila Nambiar
data_insufficient.why: Zero transcripts — didn't answer any content questions. Can't evaluate.
data_insufficient.rec: FLAG LOW — watch video or skip

data_insufficient.name: Chalika Oliver
data_insufficient.why: No Q3 transcript, Q4/Q5 are one line each. No experience data.

data_insufficient.name: Nyla Laws
data_insufficient.why: Q3 is 'Testing testing 123, can you hear me?' — not a real answer.

arizona_specific.note: Arizona is the strictest market — must have worked in a child care center, daycare, or preschool. Nannying does NOT count per AZ regulations.
arizona_specific.auto_approve: Has 6+ months specifically in a child care center, daycare, or preschool
arizona_specific.needs_verification: Claims center experience but duration unclear, or has education degree but unclear if practicum hours count
arizona_specific.likely_deny: Only informal (babysitting, nannying, own kids, family). Nannying is explicitly not professional experience in AZ. No exceptions unless Erica overrides.
edge_cases: Education internship/practicum in a school/center = may count (structured classroom hours)
Swim instructor / dance teacher = FLAG — Erica decides (not a childcare center)
Church Sunday school = FLAG — Erica decides (not a licensed center)
School aide / paraprofessional in a school = FLAG — Erica decides (school, not childcare center)

georgia_specific.note: Georgia is moderate — prefers formal experience but not as strict as Arizona
georgia_specific.auto_approve: Any amount of formal childcare (daycare, school, camp, afterschool program)
georgia_specific.judgment_call: Extensive informal (5+ years babysitting/nannying), or mix of informal + education major
georgia_specific.likely_flag: Only babysitting/family care with no professional setting

utah_specific.note: Utah is the most lenient — babysitting and family care can qualify
utah_specific.auto_approve: Pretty much anyone with childcare experience of any kind
utah_specific.judgment_call: Zero experience at all (no kids, no babysitting, nothing)
utah_specific.rarely_flag: Utah almost never flags unless the person has literally zero interaction with children
